mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals expanding consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day SPX return is below 0.10, or the signal produces negative average returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% indicates labor market weakening, reducing consumer income and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and leads to declining S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE triggers (>4.5%), the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 4% and housing starts are below 1200 units, tight monetary policy is constraining housing activity, signaling broader economic slowdown that depresses equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative or the positive proportion exceeds 40%.

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle expansion, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 0.5% month-over-month for three consecutive months
failure_condition: If the signal triggers but next-month SPX return is negative in more than 60% of instances, or if the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain below 100,000 indicates a softening labor market, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day return after negative PAYEMS shocks is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or the win rate exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising above 2% year-over-year while retail sales (RSAFS) grow less than 0.5% month-over-month, real consumer purchasing power is eroding, which depresses corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: In an early tightening cycle when the federal funds rate is above 4% but unemployment remains below 5%, the Fed is raising rates into a still-strong labor market, which historically precedes a market downturn as liquidity drains and valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The average 20-day return during triggered periods is not negative, or the negative hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate healthy construction activity and consumer confidence, which drives economic growth and upward earnings revisions, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST monthly change and next 5-day SPX return is less than 0.05 on a rolling 3-year basis.
